Efficiently and Effectively Supporting Organisations Anywhere, Anytime
----------------------------------------------------------------------

We’re a leading provider of expert modelling solutions and training, boasting a team with over 100 years of combined experience.

Our diverse backgrounds ensure innovative, top-tier results for our clients. We offer comprehensive training and resources, including a vast library of thought leadership articles, courses, and videos.

Additionally, we host the annual Excel Virtually Global event, attracting over 50 speakers and 3,500 attendees. 

[Contact us](https://sumproduct.com/contact/)

CRaFT, Our Proprietary System
-----------------------------

We believe a good financial model should exhibit four key qualities: consistency, robustness, flexibility, and transparency.

**C**onsistency is achieved through structured modelling, which reduces errors and enhances reliability.

**R**obustness is ensured by keeping models simple, strong, and auditable, eliminating hidden complexities and incorporating error checks.

**F**lexibility is provided by interactive insights, allowing key assumptions to be adjusted within defined boundaries for deeper exploration.

**T**ransparency is maintained through clear structures and consistent formulas, fostering trust in the model’s results.

[Learn more about CraFT](https://sumproduct.com/craft)

[Home](https://sumproduct.com/)

\> CRaFT

CRaFT
=====

CRaFT
-----

We help organisations redefine their direction, and improve performance. Our consultants bring deep industry knowledge and a results-oriented mindset.

[About](https://sumproduct.com/about)

[Value Proposition](https://sumproduct.com/value-proposition)

[CRaFT](https://sumproduct.com/craft)

[Books](https://sumproduct.com/published-books)

[Biographies](https://sumproduct.com/biographies)

Consistency
-----------

Structured modeling offers a win-win for both model developers and users. By enforcing consistent formatting and formula application, it significantly reduces errors, leading to more reliable and trustworthy models. Users can then grasp the model’s logic faster due to its clear and organized structure.  
  
For developers, pre-defined styles and the ability to apply formulas uniformly across data sets streamline the building process, saving time and effort. This structured approach ensures both the accuracy and speed needed to build robust and dependable models.

Robustness
----------

For bulletproof models, think “simple, strong, auditable.” This philosophy prioritises clear structure, consistent formulas, and built-in safeguards.  
  
By eliminating hidden complexities like macros, the model becomes readily auditable and mathematically accurate. Inherent error checks and sensitivity analyses further enhance robustness. This approach ensures models are not just error-free, but also transparent and reliable, fostering trust in their results.

Flexibility
-----------

Traditional models offer a single answer, leaving you wondering “what if?” Structured models break this mold with a built-in “what if?” engine.

**Flexibility for Exploration:** These models allow you to adjust key assumptions within defined boundaries. Imagine adjusting variables like interest rates or sales forecasts – all within a controlled environment.

**Unlocking Deeper Insights:** This flexibility isn’t passive. You can actively explore different scenarios and see how the model responds. This goes beyond calculations, helping you identify potential risks and opportunities for informed decision-making.

Transparency
------------

Many modellers often forget that key decision makers base their choices on printed material.

Models need to be clear, concise and cohesive.  Assumptions, calculations and outputs need to be recognisable instantly and user-friendly bearing in mind:

*   the logical flow and dynamic linking of the model
*   the number of categories required for particular calculations
*   the nature of the assumptions together with their assumption entry methods at the design stage

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

Dr. Liam Bastick
----------------

Liam has over 30 years’ experience in financial model development / auditing, valuations, M&A, strategy, training and consultancy. He has considerable experience in many different sectors (e.g. banking, energy, media, mining, oil and gas, private equity, transport and utilities). 

Liam has headed Ernst & Young’s modelling team in Melbourne and was an Assistant Director in their strategic valuations team in London. He was also a senior member of the UK Post Office’s M&A and strategy teams and has worked for / assisted various other Australian modelling companies including BPM, Corality, Navigator Project Finance, PKF and SumProduct.

He has worked in the UK, Australia, Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Hong Kong, Indonesia, Malaysia, New Zealand, Switzerland and Vietnam, with many internationally recognised clients, constructing and reviewing strategic, operational and valuation models for many high-profile IPOs, LBOs and strategic assignments. He is a regular contributor to the Institute of Chartered Accountants in Australia (ICAA), Certified Practising Accountants Australia (CPAA), the Chartered Institute of Management Accountants (CIMA), Finance 3.0 and various LinkedIn specialist discussion groups. He is also an experienced facilitator for ICAA.

Liam is a Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants (ICAEW), a Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Management Accountants (CIMA) and is a professional mathematician.

Books: An Introduction to Financial Modelling, Power BI MVP Book, Excel Insights, Continuing Financial Modelling, Financial Modelling in Power BI

Liam was the recipient of the Financial Modelling Summit’s inaugural Lifetime Achievement award in June 2021.

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

Tim Heng
--------

Tim Heng has over 20 years’ experience and is a Director in the Sydney team.  Tim has developed and reviewed financial models across a range of industries and sectors, throughout Australia and internationally.  He has worked with clients to develop bespoke modelling solutions for business challenges, using a combination of Excel, VBA and database analytics.

He has previously held analytics roles at Toyota and the Commonwealth Bank of Australia, project finance banking at OCBC Bank, and was an Associate Director at Corality Financial Group, where he managed model development and audit projects and headed up the Training Academy.

He has worked across the world, with clients in Australia, Canada, Hong Kong, Indonesia, Japan, Malaysia, New Zealand, Singapore, United Kingdom, United States and Vietnam.  These assignments have involved the development of strategic corporate and project-based financial models, model reviews, training of analysts and senior staff and include the creation of several actuarial steady-state models for modelling credit risk, provisioning and customer churn. 

Tim has written articles for the CompAct and Modelling Section newsletters published by the Society of Actuaries (US), and has been a regular presenter at conferences, training courses and webinars for Chartered Accountants Australia New Zealand (CAANZ) and CPA Australia.

Tim is also recognised as a Most Valuable Professional (MVP) in Excel by Microsoft, and one of fewer than 10 specialising in financial modelling worldwide.

**Academic qualifications:** BCom (Actuarial), BAppFin (Hons), MCom (Actuarial).

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

Oscar Hagan
-----------

Oscar Hagan has over 7 years’ experience within the finance industry.  He’s built bespoke modelling solutions across a range of industries and sectors, for clients in multiple countries throughout the UK, Europe, and Australia.  He’s provided clients with in-depth analysis, using a combination of Excel, VBA, Power BI, and various database analytics.

He has held various roles, where he’s developed bookkeeping and analytical experience across a wide range of programs.

Oscar has been involved with providing training relating to building financial models (both in Excel and Power BI), as well as numerous Excel training courses used by professional accounting bodies around the world.

![Talia Cao](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

Talia Cao
---------

Talia is a Manager of SumProduct’s Sydney team.  She’s a qualified accountant (CPA Australia) and is passionate about identifying opportunities for process improvements, analysing data to gain insights, and solving complicated Excel and Power BI problems.

Talia has worked as a project manager and as a lead modeller on a range of projects.  She has provided consulting solutions for clients from various industries to help with automating processes; building financial and data models; transforming and visualising data using the following tools.

*   Advanced Microsoft Excel (i.e. shortcut keys, Pivot Tables, Dynamic Arrays, LET/LAMBDA, Power Query, Power Pivot, VBA, Office Scripts etc.)
*   Power BI (i.e. M language, DAX, data transformation, data modelling, visualisation etc.)
*   Power Automate

In addition, Talia writes several blogs about Power BI, Power Pivot, and is mainly in charge of the Final Friday Fix & Monday Morning Mulling (_i.e._ Excel challenges) series on SumProduct website, where she covers Power BI / Excel functions as well as problems encountered when building financial models / reports and dealing with raw data.

\> Value Proposition

Value Proposition
=================

Value Proposition
-----------------

We help organisations redefine their direction, and improve performance. Our consultants bring deep industry knowledge and a results-oriented mindset.

[About](https://sumproduct.com/about)

[Value Proposition](https://sumproduct.com/value-proposition)

[CRaFT](https://sumproduct.com/craft)

[Books](https://sumproduct.com/published-books)

[Biographies](https://sumproduct.com/biographies)

We Provide Affordable, Timely Modelling Solutions
-------------------------------------------------

*   Become streamlined, yet tailored to your needs, by our modelling expert(s)
*   Make the process more transparent, consistent, flexible and robust
*   Assist in identifying or confirming key drivers of value in the process
*   Help you quantify options and alternatives
*   Reduce the likelihood of material errors in structure
*   Lessen the chances of the wrong decision(s) being made
*   Lower the financial and reputational risks for you

A Comprehensive Strategic Process
---------------------------------

Our method focuses on understanding both the current and ideal states of your organisation, evaluating all possible options, and taking decisive action. Here is an outline of the key stages of our strategic process, showing how each phase builds on the previous one to deliver a cohesive plan and business roadmap.

[Interested? Contact us](https://sumproduct.com/contact/)

1\. Parameters
--------------

Company View
------------

We begin by establishing a clear understanding of your organisation’s current position. This involves an in-depth review of your vision, mission, strategic objectives, and overall culture. By grasping the present state and aspirations of your business, we lay the groundwork for a strategy that is both relevant and forward-thinking.

Financial Analysis
------------------

A detailed financial review is undertaken to assess performance across key areas. We examine revenue streams, profitability, budgets, and forecasting data to gauge financial health. This analysis ensures that any recommended strategies are not only innovative but also economically viable, supporting sustainable growth.

Other Stakeholders
------------------

Identifying key stakeholders is essential to forming a holistic view of the business environment. This includes recognising internal influencers such as employees and management, alongside external parties like customers, suppliers, investors, and community representatives. Their insights are vital in aligning the strategic direction with broader interests and mitigating potential risks.

2\. Development
---------------

External Analysis
-----------------

This stage focuses on the market environment in which your business operates.

*   **Supply Chain Analysis:** We assess your supply chain to identify any inefficiencies or opportunities for optimisation.
*   **Competitive Forces:** By analysing the competitive landscape, we determine your market positioning relative to peers.
*   **Opportunities and Threats:** A thorough evaluation of market trends helps in recognising external opportunities and potential challenges.
*   **Market Perspective:** We study industry dynamics and customer behaviours to anticipate shifts and innovate accordingly.

Internal Analysis
-----------------

A comprehensive review of internal capabilities is crucial.

*   **Brand and Resources:** We evaluate the strength of your brand and the resources available, ensuring they support your strategic ambitions.
*   **Core Competence:** Identifying your unique strengths enables us to focus on what sets you apart in the market.
*   **Strengths and Weaknesses:** An honest assessment of internal performance helps in pinpointing areas for improvement.
*   **Management Team’s Skill Set:** We review the expertise of your leadership team, ensuring they are equipped to drive strategic initiatives.
*   **Promotion and Distribution:** An analysis of your current marketing and distribution methods informs adjustments needed to maximise market reach.

Ideal State vs Current State
----------------------------

By comparing where your organisation currently stands with where you aspire to be, we identify the gaps that need to be bridged. This gap analysis is instrumental in shaping the strategic interventions required to move from the present state to your ideal future state.

3\. Evaluation
--------------

Strategic Options
-----------------

Once the analysis is complete, we explore a range of strategic options. Each pathway is carefully reviewed to ensure it aligns with your goals and addresses identified gaps, taking into account both the opportunities and risks present in the market.

Modelling
---------

Our approach includes both quantitative and qualitative modelling techniques to simulate different scenarios. This process helps to test the viability of each strategic option, allowing us to forecast potential outcomes and fine-tune strategies before implementation.

Valuation Insights
------------------

Drawing on the modelling outcomes, we provide robust financial and operational insights. This stage is critical for assessing the return on investment and the feasibility of each strategy, ensuring that decisions are underpinned by data and aligned with long-term growth objectives.

4\. Decision
------------

Board Evaluate & Decide
-----------------------

In the decision-making phase, the strategic recommendations are presented to the leadership or board. Through a collaborative review process, the board evaluates all analyses, models, and insights to approve a plan that best meets the organisation’s needs. This stage ensures that the strategy is fully endorsed at the highest level, paving the way for successful execution.

5\. Action
----------

Strategic Plan
--------------

Following approval, we formalise a long-term strategic plan. This document encapsulates your organisation’s vision, mission, and core strategic initiatives. It serves as a blueprint for guiding sustained growth, innovation, and competitive advantage over time.

Business Plan
-------------

In parallel, a detailed business plan is developed to translate the strategic vision into operational terms. This plan outlines the specific steps, financial targets, and tactical initiatives required to implement the strategy effectively. It includes clear timelines, resource allocations, and measurable milestones to ensure accountability and track progress.

In-House Training

In-House Training
-----------------

We help organisations redefine their direction, and improve performance. Our consultants bring deep industry knowledge and a results-oriented mindset.

Tailored Training for Success
-----------------------------

The key to having a successful, high performing team is to ensure that they have the right skills and resources to draw on. We believe that the best training courses focus on practical issues faced by business professionals on a day-to-day basis. Such courses are most effectively delivered by experienced professionals who can share their broad and deep knowledge, gathered from across many industries and sectors.

SumProduct can work with your business teams to identify the key learning areas that need to be addressed, and customise a course and delivery structure that works for your needs.

Some of the advantages of an in-house training course include:

*   Case studies used in the training can be adapted to match your business needs.
*   We tailor the training materials to focus on your specific requirements.
*   It is a cost-effective means of training a larger group of people (4 or more).
*   Flexible delivery arrangements – we can work on your schedules and adapt to the needs of your team – days in a row, split across weeks, breakfast or lunch sessions, etc..

[Contact us](https://sumproduct.com/contact/)

Customised Training with ADDIE
------------------------------

We use an ADDIE approach to developing our tailored training materials, involving the following processes:

*   **Analyse** – we will sit and work with business teams, managers and HR to ensure that key training objectives will be achieved.
*   **Design** – drawing from our experience working with companies across Australia and around the globe, we will design a course structure and timetable that works for your team.
*   **Develop** – compiling materials from our wide range of public courses, and developing bespoke material on a case-by-case basis, our experts will customise a case study for your business needs.
*   **Implement** – each of our trainers has over 10 years of professional experience, and have trained thousands of students across a wide range of companies and industries.
*   **Evaluate** – after each course, we work with you to evaluate the learning experience for your team, in order to improve our services and to adapt any further courses we run for your business in line with the feedback that we get.

Our senior trainers have managed training departments at our competitors, and have developed customised training programmes for companies including NAB, ANZ, Bankwest, PPB Advisory, Australand and Leighton Contractors, amongst many others both domestically and internationally.

[Contact us](https://sumproduct.com/contact/)

\> Privacy Policy

Privacy Policy
==============

Privacy Policy
--------------

1\. Introduction
----------------

Welcome to SumProduct.com (“we”, “us” or “our”). This Privacy Policy outlines how we collect, use, disclose and protect your personal information when you visit or use our website. By accessing or using our website, you acknowledge that you have read, understood and agree to the collection, storage, use and disclosure of your personal information in accordance with this Privacy Policy.

2\. Information We Collect
--------------------------

We may collect various types of personal information, including but not limited to:

*   **Personal Identification Information:** such as your name, email address, telephone number, and other information you voluntarily provide when registering or communicating with us.
*   **Technical Information:** including your IP address, browser type, device information and browsing data that is automatically collected when you access our website.
*   **Usage Information:** details about your interactions with our website, such as pages visited, time spent on pages, and any other similar data.

3\. How We Use Your Information
-------------------------------

We use the information we collect for the following purposes:

*   **To Provide and Improve Our Services:** ensuring that our website operates effectively and is continually enhanced to better serve your needs.
*   **Communication:** to send you updates, respond to your queries, and provide customer support.
*   **Analytics:** to analyse user behaviour, monitor website performance and enhance our content and features.
*   **Legal Obligations:** to comply with applicable legal requirements and to protect our rights and interests.

4\. Cookies and Tracking Technologies
-------------------------------------

We use cookies and similar tracking technologies to:

*   Enhance your user experience.
*   Monitor and analyse website usage and trends.
*   Personalise content and advertisements.

You may adjust your browser settings to refuse cookies; however, please note that certain parts of the website may not function properly as a result.

5\. Disclosure of Your Information
----------------------------------

We may share your personal information with:

*   **Service Providers:** trusted third parties who assist us in operating our website, conducting our business, or servicing you, provided that they agree to keep this information confidential.
*   **Legal Authorities:** where required by law or in response to a valid request from law enforcement agencies.
*   **Business Transfers:** in the event of a merger, acquisition, or asset sale, your personal information may be transferred as a business asset.

We will not sell, trade, or rent your personal information to third parties for their marketing purposes without your explicit consent.

6\. International Data Transfers
--------------------------------

As a global website, your information may be transferred to, stored, and processed in countries outside your own. We take appropriate steps to ensure that your personal information remains protected in accordance with this Privacy Policy, including implementing adequate safeguards as required by applicable data protection laws.

7\. Data Security
-----------------

We are committed to ensuring that your information is secure. We employ a variety of security measures, including administrative, technical and physical controls, to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, alteration, disclosure or destruction. However, please be aware that no security measures are perfect or impenetrable.

8\. Data Retention
------------------

We retain your personal information for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes outlined in this Privacy Policy, unless a longer retention period is required or permitted by law. When the data is no longer required, it will be securely disposed of.

9\. Your Rights
---------------

Depending on your location, you may have certain rights regarding your personal information, including:

*   The right to access the personal data we hold about you.
*   The right to request correction of any inaccurate or incomplete information.
*   The right to request deletion of your personal data under certain circumstances.
*   The right to object to or restrict our processing of your data.

To exercise any of these rights, please contact us using the details provided in Section 12.

10\. Third Party Links
----------------------

Our website may contain links to third-party websites. This Privacy Policy does not apply to such external websites. We encourage you to review the privacy policies of any third-party sites you visit.

11\. Changes to This Privacy Policy
-----------------------------------

We reserve the right to update or modify this Privacy Policy at any time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated revision date. Your continued use of the website following any changes indicates your acceptance of the revised policy.

12\. Contact Us
---------------

If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us at:

**Email:** [contact@sumproduct.com](mailto:contact@sumproduct.com)

\> AI Blog: How to Improve Professional PowerPoint Slides Generated by NotebookLM

*   March 13, 2026

AI Blog: How to Improve Professional PowerPoint Slides Generated by NotebookLM
==============================================================================

_Welcome back to our AI blog series! In the previous blog, we showed how NotebookLM can turn a written paper into a structured slide deck in minutes. Using the previous article, “Audit in an AI World”, as the source, NotebookLM generated a coherent presentation that closely followed the logic, headings and messaging of the original document._

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

This first version is impressive, but it is not yet ready to go.  
We will go one step further and look at how to improve AI-generated slides, using practical examples from our own workflow. The core lesson is simple: the quality of the output is directly linked to the quality of the sources and the precision of the prompt.

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

**_Improving Slide Quality Starts with Better Sources_**  
NotebookLM does not “design” in the abstract. It learns from what we give it. Let me explain.  
In our first pass, the sources were content-focused: a Word document explaining audit risk, AI hallucination and assurance frameworks. This was enough to produce a well-structured deck, but not enough to reflect our required visual standards. To improve the visual and stylistic quality, we added another type of source, our SumProduct PowerPoint template that has been converted into PDF format.  
At the time of writing, NotebookLM does not support PPT or PPTX uploads. However, it reads PDFs very effectively. By exporting our internal slide template to PDF, including colour palette, typography, spacing and layout rules, we effectively showed NotebookLM how SumProduct slides should look.

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

This simple workaround made a noticeable difference. The regenerated deck aligned far more closely with our brand style, without requiring extensive manual reformatting afterwards. Therefore, if you want AI-generated slides to match your corporate identity, upload your slide template as a PDF source before generating the deck.

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

**_Using More Detailed Prompts to Guide the Output_**

The second improvement came from refining the prompt.

In the initial run, the instruction was deliberately simple: generate a slide deck based upon the uploaded sources.  For the next iteration, we added explicit guidance, including:

*   target audience (finance professionals and decision-makers)
*   slide tone (professional and instructional, not marketing-heavy)
*   preference for concise bullet points over dense paragraphs
*   alignment with SumProduct’s visual style.

NotebookLM responds well to this level of direction.  It does not simply summarise content; it reshapes it to fit the requested format.  This is particularly important when turning long-form analysis into presentation-friendly language.

****_The Reality Check: Spelling Errors and Locked PDFs_****

One limitation became clear once the deck was generated.

NotebookLM exports slides as PDF only.  This is convenient for sharing, but it also means the file is not directly editable.  In our example, we noticed several minor spelling, grammatical and wording errors.  Individually, they were small.  Collectively, they mattered.

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

There are two \[2\] practical ways to deal with this.  
The first approach is preventative. Fix the problem at the source: the prompt. By explicitly instructing NotebookLM to prioritise spelling and language accuracy, the model becomes noticeably more careful. This does not guarantee perfection, but it significantly reduces errors. For professional, client-facing decks, this step is worth including every time.  
The second approach is corrective. Convert the PDF into an editable deck.

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

If the slides already exist and need editing, tools such as Canva Pro or Codia AI can convert a PDF back into an editable PowerPoint file.  We tested this approach and recovered a deck that preserved most layouts and visuals, allowing us to correct text, adjust spacing and make final refinements before presentation.

One impressive outcome of this exercise was how closely the slides followed the original “_Audit in an AI World_” article.  The structure, examples and conceptual flow from AI risk and hallucination through to assurance frameworks were consistently preserved across versions.

This reinforces an important point: NotebookLM does not invent narratives.  When the source material is strong, the slides remain faithful to it.

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

**_Word to the Wise_**

NotebookLM is already capable of producing high-quality presentation drafts.  With a little extra care such as better sources, clearer prompts and a practical plan for final edits, it can become a reliable part of a professional presentation workflow.

AI will not replace judgment, proofreading or brand ownership.  However, it can remove hours of manual effort and allow us to focus on what matters most: clarity, insight and credibility.

Keep reading!  The next blog will cover audio summaries, interactive presentations, and ways NotebookLM supports various learning and communication styles. 

_Join us next time for more exciting news of AI tools!_
